title: Rethinking Cardiometabolic Risk: The Role of Childhood Neglect and Biomarker Decoupling.
abstract: Childhood adversity, including neglect, affects over 60% of children globally and fundamentally alters developing biological systems, including the cardiometabolic system. While research has established links between early neglect and disease risk, how neglect might disrupt the coherence and organization of cardiometabolic indicators contributing to risk has been overlooked.
